volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 4.1.749 - Section 5.1.846)

sections.label: Section 4.1.749
sections.original: Horrescet faxo lena , leges cum audiet .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make the procuress be terrified when she hears the conditions .

sections.label: Section 4.1.750
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , proceed and read them over to me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.750b
sections.translation: Are you attending ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.750c
sections.translation: I ’ m all attention .

sections.label: Section 4.1.751
sections.translation: Diabolus , the son of Glaucus , has made a present to Cleaereta , the procuress , of twenty silver minae , that Philenium may be with him night and day for this whole year .

sections.label: Section 4.1.754b
sections.original: Neque cum quiquam alio qui Demaenetvs
sections.translation: Yes , and not with any other person .

sections.label: Section 4.1.755
sections.translation: Am I to add that ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.755b
sections.original: Ádde , et scribas víde plane et probe .
sections.translation: Add it , and take care and write it plainly and distinctly .

sections.label: Section 4.1.756
sections.translation: And not admit any other man whatever , because either her friend or her patron , she may choose to call him —

sections.label: Section 4.1.758
sections.original: aut quód illa amicae ⋯ amatorem praedicet ,
sections.translation: Or because she may say that he is the lover of a female friend of hers . The door must be closed to all men except to yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.1.760
sections.original: in foribus scribat occupatam ésse se .
sections.translation: On the door she must write that she is engaged . Or , because she may affirm that the letter has been brought from abroad , there is not to be even any letter in the house , nor so much as a waxed tablet ; and if there is any useless picture [*] , let her sell it ; if she does not part with it , within four days

sections.label: Section 4.1.765
sections.original: abalienarit , quo abs te argentum acceperit ,
sections.translation: from the time when she has received the money of you , let it be considered as your own ; you to burn it if you like ; so that she may have no wax , with which she may be able to make a letter . She is to invite no guest ; you are to invite them . On no one of them is she to cast her eyes :

sections.label: Section 4.1.770
sections.original: si quem alium aspexit , caeca continuo siet .
sections.translation: if she looks upon any other person , she must be blind forthwith [*] . Then she is to drink cup by cup equally with yourself . She is to receive it from you ; she is to hand it to you for you to drink . She is not to have a relish for less or for more than yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.1.773b
sections.translation: That ’ s quite to my taste .

sections.label: Section 4.1.774
sections.translation: She is to remove all causes of suspicion from her ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.775
sections.original: neque illaec ulli pede pedém homini premat ,
sections.translation: nor is she to tread on any man ’ s foot [*] with her foot ; when she rises she is neither to step upon the next couch , nor when she gets down from the couch is she thence to extend her hand to any one ; she is not to give to nor ask of any one a ring for her to look at ; she is not to present dice to any man whatever except to yourself ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.780
sections.original: cum iaciat , te ne dicat : nomen nominet .
sections.translation: when she throws [*] them , she is not to say , ’ You I call upon ,’ she is to mention your name . She may call on any Goddess that she pleases as propitious to her , but on no God : if she should chance to be very full of devotion , she is to tell you , and you are to pray to him that he may be propitious . She is neither to nod at any man , wink , or make a sign .

sections.label: Section 4.1.785
sections.original: post , si lucerna exstincta sit , ne quid sui
sections.translation: In fine , if the lamp goes out , she is not to move a single joint of herself in the dark .

sections.label: Section 4.1.786b
sections.translation: That ’ s very good ; so , in fact , she must do : but expunge that about the chamber ; for my part , I prefer that she should move . I don ’ t wish her to have an excuse , and to say that it is forbidden her by her vow .

sections.label: Section 4.1.790
sections.translation: I understand , you fear some quibble .

sections.label: Section 4.1.790b
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 4.1.790c
sections.translation: Then as you bid me , I ’ ll strike it out .

sections.label: Section 4.1.791b
sections.translation: And why not ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.791c
sections.translation: Hear the rest .

sections.label: Section 4.1.791d
sections.translation: Say on , I ’ m listening .

sections.label: Section 4.1.792
sections.translation: And she is not to use any shuffling words , nor is she to know how to speak in any tongue but the Attic . If perchance she should begin to cough ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.795
sections.original: ut cuiquam linguam in tussiendo proserat .
sections.translation: she is not to cough so as to expose her tongue to any one in coughing . But if she should pretend as though she had a running at the nose [*] , even then she is not to do so ; you yourself must wipe her lips rather than that she should open her mouth before another person . And her mother , the procuress , is not to come in in the middle of the wine ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.800
sections.original: nec ulli verbo male dicat . si dixerit ,
sections.translation: nor is she to utter a word of abuse to any one ; if she does so speak , let this be her fine , to go for twenty days without wine .

sections.label: Section 4.1.802b
sections.translation: You have written it nicely ; a clever agreement .

sections.label: Section 4.1.803
sections.original: “ Tum si coronas , serta , unguenta iusserit
sections.translation: Then , if she bids her maid-servant carry chaplets , garlands , or unguents , to Venus or to Cupid ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.805
sections.original: tuos servos servet , Venerine eas det an viro .
sections.translation: your servant is to watch whether she gives them to Venus or to a man . If perchance she should say she wishes to keep herself in purity [*] , let her account for as many nights as she has kept herself in purity . These are no trifles ; for they are no funeral dirge [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.1.809
sections.translation: The conditions please me entirely ; follow me indoors .

sections.label: Section 4.1.809b
sections.translation: I follow .

sections.label: Section 4.2.810
sections.original: Sequere hac . egone haec patiar aut taceam ? emori
sections.translation: Follow this way . Am I to put up with this , or shall I hold my tongue ? I would rather die than not discover this to his wife . And say you so , old man ? With a mistress would you be acting the part of a youngster ? Would you be excusing yourself to your wife , and calling yourself an aged man ? Would you be taking the mistress from her lover ? And would you be presenting the money

sections.label: Section 4.2.815
sections.original: lenae ? suppiles clam domi uxorem tuam ?
sections.translation: to the procuress , and be secretly pilfering it from your wife at home ? You should hang me , rather than you should carry off these matters undiscovered . On my honor , I ’ ll really go this instant hence to her whom I ’ m sure that you ’ ll very soon be destroying , in order that you may be able to supply your extravagance , unless , indeed , she shall first prevent you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.820
sections.original: Ego sic faciundum censeo : me honestiust ,
sections.translation: I ’ m of opinion that thus you must act . ’ Tis more becoming that I should disclose this matter , rather than yourself , lest she may think that you , excited by reason of love , rather than for her own sake , have acted thus .

sections.label: Section 4.2.823b
sections.translation: Why , faith , you say what ’ s right . Do you then contrive to raise a storm and strife against him ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.825
sections.original: cum suo sibí gnato unam ad amicam de die
sections.translation: that he , together with his own son , is carousing with one mistress the livelong day , and that he ’ s secretly pilfering from her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.826b
sections.translation: Don ’ t suggest to me . I ’ ll take care of that .

sections.label: Section 4.2.827b
sections.translation: But I ’ ll wait for you at home .

sections.label: Section 5.1.828
sections.translation: Come then , father , let ’ s take our places , please .

sections.label: Section 5.1.828c
sections.translation: As you bid me , my son , so it shall be .

sections.label: Section 5.1.829b
sections.translation: Lads , spread the table .

sections.label: Section 5.1.830
sections.original: Numquídnam tibi moléstumst , gnate mí , si haec nunc mecum áccubat ?
sections.translation: Is it at all displeasing to you , son , if she takes her place by me ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.831
sections.original: Pietas , pater , oculis dolorem prohibet . quamquam ego istánc amo ,
sections.translation: Duty , father , keeps sorrow from my eyes ; although I love her , still I can control my feelings , not to take it to heart because she takes her place by you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.833
sections.translation: It becomes a young man to be respectful , Argyrippus .

sections.label: Section 5.1.833b
sections.translation: Troth , father , through proper regard for you , I can be so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.834b
sections.translation: Come , then , let ’ s enjoy this banquet

sections.label: Section 5.1.835
sections.original: vino ut sermone suavi . nolo ego metui , amari mavolo ,
sections.translation: with wine and pleasant discourse . I don ’ t wish to be feared , I prefer myself to be loved by you , my son .

sections.label: Section 5.1.836b
sections.original: Pol ego utrumque facio , ut aequom est filium .
sections.translation: In truth , I do them both , as is proper for a son .

sections.label: Section 5.1.837
sections.original: Credam istuc , si esse te hilarum videro .
sections.translation: I ’ d believe it , if I saw you were cheerful .

sections.label: Section 5.1.837b
sections.translation: Why , do you think that I ’ m sorrowful ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.838
sections.original: Putem ego , quem videam aeque esse maestum ut quasi dies si dicta sit ?
sections.translation: Do I think so ? You , whom I see as melancholy as if the day [*] of trial had been named for you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.839_840
sections.translation: Don ’ t you say that .

sections.label: Section 5.1.839_840b
sections.original: Ne sic fueris : ilico ego non dixero .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be so , then I ’ ll not say so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.841
sections.translation: Well then , look at me ; I ’ m laughing .

sections.label: Section 5.1.841b
sections.original: Vtinam male qui mihi volunt sic rideant .
sections.translation: I ’ d like that those who wish me ill would laugh thus .

sections.label: Section 5.1.842
sections.original: Scio equidem quam ob rem me , pater , tu tristem credas nunc tibi :
sections.translation: I know , indeed , father , why you now suppose to yourself that I ’ m sad ; it is because she is by you . And really , father , by my faith , to tell you the truth , that matter does hurt me ; and not for this reason , that I don ’ t desire for you that which you wish ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.845
sections.original: verum istam amo . aliam tecum esse equidem facile possum perpeti .
sections.translation: but because I love her ; another woman , indeed , I could easily endure to be by you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.846
sections.translation: But I have a fancy for this one .
